prerequisites: A OneSignal account with one or more applications you want to sync.
Each application's App ID and REST API Key (app_api_key), found in the OneSignal dashboard under Settings > Keys & IDs.
Your account-level User Auth Key for account-wide access across applications.
The list of outcome names to retrieve (the outcome_names field), supplied as comma-separated values, for example os__session_duration.count,os__click.count.
A start date (start_date) in ISO 8601 format (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ) marking the earliest data to sync.

authMethods.name: Per-application REST API Key (app_api_key) + App ID
authMethods.summary: Each entry in the applications array authenticates with that application's App ID (app_id) and REST API Key (app_api_key). The App ID publicly identifies the OneSignal app, while the REST API Key is a private app-level secret used to read that app's data. Both are found in the OneSignal dashboard under Settings > Keys & IDs.

authMethods.name: Account-level User Auth Key (user_auth_key)
authMethods.summary: The user_auth_key is an account-level (organization) credential that grants access across your OneSignal applications. It is a secret and must be kept confidential. OneSignal treats this as a legacy User Auth Key; provide it from your OneSignal account settings.

setupSteps.title: Locate each application's App ID and REST API Key
setupSteps.body: In the OneSignal dashboard, open each application you want to sync and go to Settings > Keys & IDs. Copy the App ID and the REST API Key for that application. The App ID identifies the app and the REST API Key (app_api_key) is the private app-level secret used to read its data.

setupSteps.title: Obtain your account-level User Auth Key
setupSteps.body: Retrieve your User Auth Key (user_auth_key) from your OneSignal account settings. This account-level secret authorizes access across your applications. Keep it confidential and store it securely.

setupSteps.title: Add your applications
setupSteps.body: In the applications field, add one entry per OneSignal app you want to sync. For each entry provide the App ID (app_id) and REST API Key (app_api_key) collected earlier (an internal app name is used only for identification).

setupSteps.title: Specify the outcome names to retrieve
setupSteps.body: Set outcome_names to a comma-separated list of the OneSignal outcomes you want to pull, for example os__session_duration.count,os__click.count. Refer to the OneSignal API documentation for the available outcome options for your apps.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync start date
setupSteps.body: Set start_date to the earliest point from which data should be synced, formatted as an ISO 8601 timestamp (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ).
